2603.16292.title: Attention-guided Evidence Grounding for Spoken Question Answering
2603.16292.paper_summary: Spoken Question Answering (Spoken QA) presents a challenging cross-modal problem: effectively aligning acoustic queries with textual knowledge while avoiding the latency and error propagation inherent in cascaded ASR-based systems. In this paper, we introduce Attention-guided Evidence Grounding (AEG), a novel end-to-end framework that leverages the internal cross-modal attention of Speech Large Language Models (SpeechLLMs) to explicitly locate and ground key evidence in the model's latent space. To address the diffuse attention distribution in pre-trained models, we propose Learning to Focus on Evidence (LFE), a supervised fine-tuning paradigm that calibrates the model's attention mechanism to distinguish query-relevant segments from irrelevant context. Experiments on SQuAD, HotpotQA, and MuSiQue demonstrate that AEG reduces hallucinations and achieves strong efficiency gains, outperforming large-scale cascaded baselines (Whisper-Large-v3 + Reranker) while reducing inference latency by approximately 62%.
2603.16292.paper_summary_zh: 口語問題回答（Spoken QA）呈現了一個具有挑戰性的跨模態問題：有效地將聲音查詢與文本知識對齊，同時避免級聯自動語音識別（ASR）系統固有的延遲和錯誤傳播。在本文中，我們介紹了注意力引導的證據基礎（AEG），這是一個新穎的端到端框架，利用語音大型語言模型（SpeechLLMs）的內部跨模態注意力，明確定位並在模型的潛在空間中固定關鍵證據。為了解決預訓練模型中擴散的注意力分佈，我們提出了學習專注於證據（LFE），這是一種監督式微調範式，旨在調整模型的注意力機制，以區分與查詢相關的片段和不相關的上下文。對於SQuAD、HotpotQA和MuSiQue的實驗顯示，AEG減少了幻覺並實現了強大的效率提升，超越了大規模的級聯基準（Whisper-Large-v3 + Reranker），同時將推理延遲降低了約62%。
2603.16292.author: Ke Yang et.al.
2603.16292.authors: Ke Yang, Bolin Chen, Yuejie Li, Yueying Hua, Jianhao Nie, Yueping He, Bowen Li, Chengjun Mao
